prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

(register_batch_record primary_batch_1)
(reserve_line_slot_for_batch primary_batch_1 line_slot_1)
(assign_product_variant_to_batch_and_allocate_operator primary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(stage_batch_for_processing primary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(validate_equipment_and_assign_material_kit primary_batch_1 equipment_position_1 material_kit_1)
(verify_slot_and_return_material_kit primary_batch_1 equipment_position_1 product_variant_1 material_kit_1)
(unassign_operator_from_batch primary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(register_batch_record secondary_batch_1)
(reserve_line_slot_for_batch secondary_batch_1 line_slot_2)

(mark_primary_batch_ready_for_execution primary_batch_1 run_record_1)
(mark_secondary_batch_ready_for_execution secondary_batch_1 run_record_1)
(unassign_operator_from_batch primary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(assign_product_variant_to_batch_and_allocate_operator secondary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(attach_release_document_to_batch secondary_batch_1 release_document_1 product_variant_1)
(finalize_allocation_for_secondary_batch_and_release_slot secondary_batch_1 line_slot_2 release_document_1)
(unassign_operator_from_batch secondary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(assign_product_variant_to_batch_and_allocate_operator primary_batch_1 product_variant_1)
(attach_release_document_to_batch primary_batch_1 release_document_1 product_variant_1)
(finalize_allocation_and_release_slot primary_batch_1 line_slot_1 release_document_1)
(register_batch_record packaging_line_1)
(reserve_line_slot_for_batch packaging_line_1 line_slot_1)
(assign_product_variant_to_batch_and_allocate_operator packaging_line_1 product_variant_1)
(stage_batch_for_processing packaging_line_1 product_variant_1)
(bind_qa_approval_to_line packaging_line_1 qa_approval_record_1)
(reserve_component_batch_to_line packaging_line_1 component_batch_1)
(allocate_equipment_to_entity packaging_line_1 operator_1)
(assign_inspection_tool_to_run packaging_line_1 inspection_tool_1 run_record_1)
(prepare_line_for_packaging packaging_line_1 inspection_tool_1 run_record_1 product_variant_1)
(authorize_line_execution_without_label_flag packaging_line_1 component_batch_1 operator_1 inspection_tool_1 run_record_1)
(complete_packaging_checks packaging_line_1 qa_approval_record_1 inspection_tool_1 run_record_1)
(finalize_release_and_mark_execution_ready packaging_line_1)
(attach_release_document_to_batch packaging_line_1 release_document_1 product_variant_1)
(finalize_allocation_for_line_and_release_slot packaging_line_1 line_slot_1 release_document_1)
(unassign_operator_from_batch packaging_line_1 product_variant_1)
(release_equipment_from_entity packaging_line_1 operator_1)
(release_component_batch_from_line packaging_line_1 component_batch_1)
(unbind_qa_approval_from_line packaging_line_1 qa_approval_record_1)
(register_batch_record packaging_line_2)
(reserve_line_slot_for_batch packaging_line_2 line_slot_2)
(assign_product_variant_to_batch_and_allocate_operator packaging_line_2 product_variant_1)
(stage_batch_for_processing packaging_line_2 product_variant_1)
(bind_qa_approval_to_line packaging_line_2 qa_approval_record_1)
(reserve_component_batch_to_line packaging_line_2 component_batch_1)
(allocate_equipment_to_entity packaging_line_2 operator_1)
(assign_inspection_tool_to_run packaging_line_2 inspection_tool_2 run_record_1)
(prepare_line_for_packaging packaging_line_2 inspection_tool_2 run_record_1 product_variant_1)
(authorize_line_execution_without_label_flag packaging_line_2 component_batch_1 operator_1 inspection_tool_2 run_record_1)

message.reasoning:
The problem involves allocating slots for batches and lines in a packaging system. The goals are to have resource_allocated_or_locked for primary_batch_1, secondary_batch_1, packaging_line_1, and packaging_line_2.


## Examining Allocation Goals  
- The task is to allocate slots for batches and lines in a packaging system.  
- It involves ensuring resource_allocated_or_locked for primary_batch_1, secondary_batch_1, packaging_line_1, and packaging_line_2.

## Examining Initial State  
- Available slots include line_slot_1, line_slot_2, and line_slot_3 for allocation.  
- Operator_1 is available for product_variant_1, with material_kit_1 and equipment links established for primary_batch_1 and secondary_batch_1.  

## Assessing Resource Availability  
- Run_record_1 is available, and lines are linked to it, with inspection tools 1 and 2 accessible.  
- Labels 1,2, templates 1,2, release_document_1, and component_batch_1 are ready for use.  

## Evaluating Holds and Constraints  
- Quality_hold_tag_1 is present on packaging_line_1, but packaging_line_2 has no special hold despite an association with quality_hold_tag_2.  
- No batch_record_registered exists yet, impacting the locking process for resources.  

## Analyzing Locking Process  
- To lock resources, finalize_allocation_and_release_slot or similar requires documentation_attached, batch_assigned_to_slot, and release_document_bound_to.  
- Processable entities include primary_batch_1, secondary_batch_1, and packaging_lines 1 and 2, all subtypes of processable_entity.

## Reviewing Resource Assignment  
- Operator_1 and material_kit_1 are unique, with product_variant_1 assigned to one operator at a time.  
- Assigning a product variant to a batch allocates an operator, but unassigning reverses this process.  

## Assessing Holds and Line Processing  
- Quality_hold_tag_1 affects packaging_line_1, but packaging_line_2 has an association with quality_hold_tag_2 without a special hold.  
- Acknowledge_line_hold for packaging_line_1 requires entity_staged and batch_assigned_product_variant, but packaging_line_2 cannot proceed due to missing special_hold_present.  

## Planning Hold Clearance  
- Clearing holds involves recording clearance steps, binding approvals, and setting execution_readiness_flag for lines.  
- After hold clearance, attach_release_document_to_batch requires execution_readiness_flag and batch_assigned_product_variant.  

## Considering Slot Allocation  
- Both lines and batches need batch_assigned_to_slot for final allocation, even in the hold clearance path.  
- Processable entities like primary_batch_1 and secondary_batch_1 require staging via slot booking, confirmed by reserve slot.

## Examining Line Processing Paths  
- Line2 lacks special_hold_present for quality_hold_tag_2, so acknowledge_line_hold cannot proceed, unlike line1.  
- Alternative paths for line2 include packaging_checks_complete and finalize_release, requiring line_execution_authorized and run_confirmed_for_execution.  

## Analyzing Batch Readiness  
- Batches like primary_batch_1 and secondary_batch_1 require the run path to achieve execution_readiness_flag, tied to a single run_record_1.  
- Actions like mark_primary_batch_ready_for_execution need run_token_reserved, run_confirmed_for_execution, and slot check-in steps for both batches.  

## Planning Batch Preparation  
- Prepare batches by registering, reserving slots, assigning product variants, and staging, potentially using different slots for primary and secondary.  
- Stage equipment for primary_batch_1 at equip_pos1 and allocate to operator1, ensuring slot_equipment_link and equipment_staged preconditions are met.  

## Considering Operator Allocation  
- Allocate_equipment_to_entity requires equipment_unit_available, but only one operator1 is available, posing a constraint for secondary_batch_1.  
- Complete_slot_checkin_with_operator for primary_batch_1 needs entity_staged, equipment_allocated, and slot_equipment_link, but operator allocation remains a bottleneck.

## Preparing Batch Execution  
- Confirm_run_for_execution for run_record1 with primary_batch_1 requires run_token_reserved, slot_checkin_completed for primary, and batch_assigned_product_variant for primary_batch_1.  
- Mark batches ready by using mark_primary_batch_ready and mark_secondary_batch_ready, setting execution_readiness_flag without additional prerequisites.  

## Attaching Release Documents  
- Attach_release_document_to_batch for primary_batch_1 needs execution_readiness_flag, batch_assigned_product_variant, and an available release_doc1.  
- Finalize allocation for batches using finalize_allocation_and_release_slot, with different actions for primary and secondary, ensuring sequential processing due to limited release documents.  

## Configuring Line Packaging  
- Assign_inspection_tool_to_run for packaging_line requires entity_staged, line_linked_to_run, line_has_inspec, tool_available, and run_token_reserved, alongside run_confirmed_for_execution.  
- Prepare_line_for_packaging needs batch_assigned_product_variant for the line, entity_staged, and no run_quality_pending, setting line_packaging_prepared.  

## Authorizing Line Execution  
- Authorize_line_execution_without_label_flag requires line_packaging_prepared, component_reserved_to_line, equipment_allocated_to_entity, and no run_label_pending, setting line_exec_auth.  
- Complete_packaging_checks needs line_exec_auth, approval_bound_to_line, and no quality or label pending, setting packaging_checks_complete without additional checks.

## Processing Batch Staging  
- Stage batches like primary_batch_1 and secondary_batch_1 by registering them and confirming slot allocation first.  
- Assign product_variant_1 to batches, ensuring operator availability, which may limit concurrent processing.  

## Preparing Equipment Validation  
- Stage equipment for batches like primary_batch_1 using stage_equipment_for_batch_slot, requiring entity_staged and slot linkage.  
- Validate equipment and assign material kits via validate_equipment_and_assign_material_kit, setting equipment_validated without needing inspection tools for material paths.  

## Managing Line Operations  
- Reserve component batches for lines using reserve_component_batch_to_line, separate from batch readiness checks.  
- Note packaging_lines can proceed without labels since no quality or label pending, aligning with prepare_line and authorize_without_label_flag.  

## Handling Operational Constraints  
- Only one operator_1 is available initially, affecting equipment allocation for lines but not batch check-ins via material paths.  
- Packaging_lines and batches share product_variant_1, potentially creating contention, but holds on lines don’t block packaging path progress.

## Managing Product Variant Assignment
- Assigning product_variant_1 (pv1) to batches like primary_batch_1 (pri1) and secondary_batch_1 (sec1) requires operator availability, limiting concurrent assignments.
- Unassigning pv1 from pri1 frees the operator, allowing assignment to sec1, but confirming pri1’s run requires pv1 to remain assigned.

## Resolving Batch Sequencing
- Staging and verifying sec1 needs pv1 assigned, creating a conflict with pri1’s confirmation requirements.
- Sequential processing is necessary, completing one batch’s full cycle (staging, verifying, confirming) before moving to the next, as only one batch can hold pv1 at a time.

## Sequencing Batch Operations  
- Prepare primary_batch_1 (pri1) by registering, reserving slot1, and assigning product_variant_1 (pv1), then stage and validate equipment with material kits.  
- After verifying pri1, unassign pv1 to free operator availability, allowing assignment to secondary_batch_1 (sec1) for staging and validation.  

## Validating Station Processes  
- Validate stations for sec1 without requiring prior station staging, using validate_station_and_assign_material_kit, ensuring material availability and station linkage.  
- Verify stations after validation, freeing materials and marking sec1 as verified, similar to equipment validation for pri1.  

## Managing Run Confirmation  
- Reserve run tokens for pri1 and sec1, linking equipment and stations, without needing pv1 assigned during this step.  
- Confirming the run for pri1 requires pv1 to be reassigned, as unassigning during sec1 processing creates a conflict with confirmation prerequisites.

## Preparing Line Operations  
- Equipment allocation occurs for lines, not batches, with operator_1 assigned to line1, making operator_1 unavailable.  
- Inspection tool assignment to run_record_1 requires the line to be staged, linked, and the tool available, marking the tool in use.  

## Configuring Packaging Line  
- Preparing packaging_line_1 for execution involves ensuring the line is staged, the tool is in use, and product_variant_1 is assigned, marking the line as prepared.  
- Authorizing line execution requires the line prepared, component reserved, and tool linked, resulting in execution authorization.  

## Finalizing Packaging Checks  
- Completing packaging checks needs execution authorization, QA approval, and tool linkage, marking checks as complete.  
- Finalizing release and marking execution ready happens without additional checks, setting the line as ready and finalized.  

## Managing Line Resources  
- Attaching release documents to the line requires execution readiness and document availability, binding the document and making it unavailable.  
- Finalizing allocation releases the slot and document, locking the line for further operations.  

## Releasing Line Resources  
- Releasing equipment, components, and QA approvals from line1 makes them available for line2, ensuring resource availability.  
- Unassigning product_variant_1 from line1 frees operator availability, preparing for the next line’s setup.

## Processing Line Setup  
- Registering and reserving line_slot_1 for packaging_line_1 initiates the process, ensuring the line is ready.  
- Assigning product_variant_1 and staging the batch sets up packaging_line_1 for further operations.  

## Configuring Line Resources  
- Binding QA approval and reserving component_batch_1 to packaging_line_1 prepares for execution.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
